# Security Policy

## Supported Versions

I am committed to keeping pysphinx_mcp secure.
At this time, I only provide security updates and patches for the [latest stable release](https://github.com/vmphase/pysphinx_mcp/releases/latest).
If you are using an outdated version of the package, I strongly recommend upgrading to the most recent version to ensure you have the latest security fixes.

## Reporting a Vulnerability

**Please do not report security vulnerabilities through public GitHub issues.**

If you discover a potential security flaw in pysphinx_mcp (e.g., unsafe handling of remote content, path traversal in page fetching, or crashing vulnerabilities), please report it via one of the following methods:

1.  **GitHub Security Advisory:** Navigate to the [Security tab](https://github.com/vmphase/pysphinx_mcp/security/advisories/new) of this repository and select "Report a vulnerability". This is the preferred method as it allows for a private discussion, collaborative fixing, and a formal CVE assignment if necessary.
2.  **Private Contact:** You may contact me directly at [contact method].

### My Response Process

Once a report is received:
- I will acknowledge the report within **48 hours**.
- I will work on a fix in a private, secure environment.
- A security advisory and a patched version will be published simultaneously to notify the community.

## Disclosure Policy

I ask that you do not disclose the vulnerability publicly until a fix has been released. This helps protect users who rely on pysphinx_mcp for browsing and processing documentation content.
